problem_statement: Knowledge base search is too restrictive: it appears to require a query match in topic, title, and content at the same time, so articles matching only one field are filtered out.
goal: Change knowledge base search so a query matches if it appears in any one of topic, title, or content, and return those articles as search results.
acceptance_criteria: Searching by a term present only in the title returns the article.
Searching by a term present only in the topic returns the article.
Searching by a term present only in the content returns the article.
Existing behavior still returns matching articles when multiple fields contain the term.
constraints: Scope should be limited to the knowledge search implementation in backend/app/services/knowledge_service.py unless supporting query-building code is required.
Behavior change should preserve current search semantics aside from switching AND-style filtering to OR-style filtering.
unknowns: Whether the search is implemented as ORM filters, raw SQL, or a helper method inside knowledge_service.py.
Whether any tests already cover search behavior and need updating.
repo_summary: Backend service-oriented repository; knowledge search logic is likely in backend/app/services/knowledge_service.py, with route/schema/model support around backend/app/api/routes/knowledge.py and backend/app/models/knowledge_article.py.

targeted_symbols.reason: Issue description explicitly identifies the search condition bug and the affected file points here; this is the smallest likely implementation area.
Likely location to add or adjust coverage for OR-vs-AND search behavior.

localization_rationale: The bug report directly names backend/app/services/knowledge_service.py and specifically the search behavior.
The symptom is a boolean logic error in query composition (AND instead of OR) rather than a model or route issue.
Only a small service-level change is likely needed, with tests in the corresponding service test file to prevent regression.
